model_output:
Based on the `UsersController.scala` file I read, here's the **User data model**:

## User Case Class

```scala
case class User(id: Int, name: String, email: String, createdAt: LocalDateTime)
```

### Fields:

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|------------|------|-------------|
| `id` | `Int` | The unique identifier for the user |
| `name` | `String` | The user's name |
| `email` | `String` | The user's email address |
| `createdAt` | `java.time.LocalDateTime` | Timestamp when the user was created |

The file also shows example instances of `User`:
- User(1, "Alice", "alice@example.com", 2024-01-15 10:30)
- User(2, "Bob", "bob@example.com", 2024-02-20 14:00)
- User(3, "Charlie", "charlie@example.com", 2024-03-05 09:15)
